Practice Management

cash pay therapy practice software

Last updated: January 15, 2025 • 5 min read

cash pay therapy practice software

Running a cash-pay practice sounds simple until you’re stuck exporting superbills, chasing unpaid invoices, and juggling three apps just to get paid. You didn’t get into therapy to become a part-time biller, so let’s fix that. Below I compare the two most common EHRs therapists actually use for self-pay work: SimplePractice and TheraNest. I’ll show you exactly how each handles rates, cards, portals, and—most importantly—your time.

Quick verdict:
SimplePractice gives you the slickest client portal and autopay tools; TheraNest costs a dollar less per month and won’t cap your file storage. Pick the one that matches your budget and tolerance for dated interfaces.

SimplePractice

Pricing

  • Solo – $39/mo (annual)
  • Telehealth – extra $10/mo
  • Card processing – 2.95% + 30¢ per transaction

Cash-pay features you’ll use every day

  • Separate self-pay fee schedule (insurance rates never bleed through)
  • Auto-generated superbills with CPT & ICD-10 codes
  • Credit-card on file + recurring autopay
  • Client portal for booking, payment, and intake forms
  • Automated billing reminders with optional late-fee scheduling

Pros

  • Intuitive, modern interface—clients rarely call for help
  • Robust mobile app lets you run the practice from your phone
  • Accepts Stripe, HSA/FSA cards, Google Pay, Apple Pay out of the box

Cons

  • Document templates aren’t fully customizable—some still use Word
  • Telehealth add-on pushes monthly cost higher than advertised

Best for

Therapists who want a premium client experience and are willing to pay an extra dollar (and a slightly higher card rate) for automation that saves 30–60 billing minutes a week.
Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free →

TheraNest

Pricing

  • Solo – $38/mo (annual)
  • Telehealth – extra $8/mo
  • Card processing – flat 2.9% via Payrix

Cash-pay features you’ll use every day

  • Unlimited self-pay service codes—no nickel-and-diming
  • Batch invoicing & statements (great for monthly package clients)
  • Superbill generator with editable CPT/ICD lines
  • Integrated Payrix credit-card processing
  • Client balance alerts & payment-plan tracking

Pros

  • Lowest per-provider cost for groups—sliding scale down to $14/provider
  • Unlimited document storage; stop deleting old files
  • Built-in Wiley Planners templates if you like structured notes

Cons

  • Interface feels 2012; expect more clicks
  • No native client portal—booking links rely on third-party calendars

Best for

Cost-aware therapists who need unlimited file storage, bulk billing, and don’t mind a dated UI.
Try THERANEST free →

SimplePractice vs. TheraNest at a glance

FeatureSimplePracticeTheraNest
Self-pay rate managementYesYes
Superbill creationYesYes
Credit-card processingStripe 2.95% + 30¢Payrix 2.9%
Client portalYesThird-party only
Telehealth$10/mo add-on$8/mo add-on
Monthly base price (solo)$39$38

Who should choose which

Choose SimplePractice if you:

  • Want clients to book, pay, and sign forms in one slick portal
  • Rely on autopay and automated reminders to keep cash flow steady
  • Don’t mind paying a tiny bit more per swipe for a modern UI

Choose TheraNest if you:

  • Store tons of assessments and need unlimited file space
  • Run a group practice and want the lowest per-clinician fee
  • Prefer batch invoicing 50 clients in two clicks
  • Don’t care that the interface looks older than your couch

FAQ

What is the best cash pay therapy practice software for solo therapists?

SimplePractice edges out for user experience and autopay; TheraNest wins on price and storage. Decide if you value looks or savings more.

How do I create a superbill in SimplePractice for self-pay clients?

After the session, click “Generate Superbill,” review auto-populated CPT/ICD codes, and email directly to the client—no manual typing.

Can TheraNest handle credit-card processing for private-pay therapy?

Yes, via Payrix at 2.9%. Store the card at intake, then charge per session or bulk invoice monthly packages.

Is there an EHR that charges no extra fees for self-pay documentation?

Both SimplePractice and TheraNest include superbills, self-pay rate tables, and statements in the base plan—no surprise surcharges.

How can I reduce no-shows in a cash-only therapy practice?

Collect card details at intake, enable autopay, and turn on automated reminders. Clients are less likely to ghost when their card is already on file.

Conclusion

You didn’t sign up to chase invoices. Both SimplePractice and TheraNest handle the core cash-pay tasks—superbills, self-pay rates, card processing—without hidden fees. If you want the most automated, client-friendly experience, SimplePractice is worth the extra dollar. If every penny counts and you need unlimited storage, grab TheraNest. Either way, pick one, turn on autopay, and get back to doing the work you actually love.
Try SIMPLEPRACTICE free → Try THERANEST free →
